# eximIA Integration Contract v1 — Full Specification

## Contract ID
eximia-integration/v1

## Description
Universal contract for inter-app integration in the eximIA ecosystem. Any app implementing these endpoints becomes automatically connectable to any other — present or future — with zero hardcoding, no intermediary, and no package dependency.

## Base Path
/api/v1/integration

---

## Authentication

Type: API Key
Header: x-eximia-api-key
Format: eximia_<app>_<random32>
Hash: SHA-256 (never store raw key)

### Scopes
- read: List and fetch entities (GET)
- write: Create and update records (POST, PUT)
- admin: Full access + webhooks + configuration

Each app manages its own API keys. The Hub does NOT centralize key management.

---

## Endpoints

### GET /catalog
Returns app metadata, exposed entities with schemas, operations, and webhook events.
Auth: required (scope: read)

Response:
```json
{
  "app": "string — app identifier slug",
  "version": "string — implementation version",
  "contract": "eximia-integration/v1",
  "entities": {
    "<entity_name>": {
      "operations": ["list", "get", "create", "update"],
      "schema": {
        "<field>": { "type": "string|number|boolean|object|array|datetime", "readonly": true, "required": true, "description": "..." }
      },
      "description": "string"
    }
  },
  "webhooks": {
    "available_events": ["entity.action"]
  }
}
```

### GET /:entity
Lists paginated data of an entity.
Auth: required (scope: read)
Query: page (default 1), limit (default 20, max 100)

Response:
```json
{
  "data": [...],
  "meta": { "total": 100, "page": 1, "limit": 20, "pages": 5 }
}
```

### POST /:entity
Creates a record.
Auth: required (scope: write)
Body: entity fields per schema (excluding readonly)

Response:
```json
{ "data": { ...created record } }
```

### GET /:entity/:id
Fetches individual record.
Auth: required (scope: read)

Response:
```json
{ "data": { ...record } }
```

### PUT /:entity/:id
Updates individual record.
Auth: required (scope: write)
Body: partial entity fields (excluding readonly)

Response:
```json
{ "data": { ...updated record } }
```

---

## Errors

Format:
```json
{
  "error": "human-readable message",
  "code": "MACHINE_CODE",
  "details": {}
}
```

Codes:
- UNAUTHORIZED (401): Missing or invalid API key
- FORBIDDEN (403): Key lacks required scope
- ENTITY_NOT_FOUND (404): Entity not exposed
- RECORD_NOT_FOUND (404): Record not found
- VALIDATION_ERROR (422): Body validation failed
- RATE_LIMITED (429): Too many requests
- INTERNAL_ERROR (500): Unexpected error

---

## Webhooks

Registration: POST /api/v1/integration/webhooks
Signature header: x-eximia-signature
Algorithm: HMAC-SHA256
Retry: 3 attempts (1s, 5s, 30s exponential backoff)
On 3 consecutive failures: webhook auto-disabled

Payload:
```json
{
  "event": "entity.action",
  "app": "source-app",
  "entity": "entity_name",
  "record_id": "uuid",
  "data": { ...record },
  "timestamp": "ISO 8601"
}
```

Signature verification:
```
expected = HMAC-SHA256(secret, JSON.stringify(body))
valid = header === "sha256=" + expected
```

---

## Versioning

Header: x-eximia-contract-version
Current: v1
Policy: Backward-compatible additions only. Breaking changes require new version.

---

## Admin Panel Implementation

Every app MUST have /settings/integrations with:

### Architecture
- INBOUND: Other apps call YOUR endpoints. Managed via API keys you create for them.
- OUTBOUND: YOU call other apps. Managed via connections storing remote URL + encrypted key.

### Database Tables

#### integration_keys (inbound auth)
- id: uuid primary key
- app_name: text not null
- key_prefix: text not null (first 16 chars)
- key_hash: text not null unique (SHA-256)
- scopes: text[] default '{read}'
- status: text default 'active' (active|revoked)
- last_used: timestamptz
- expires_at: timestamptz
- created_at: timestamptz

#### integration_outbound (outbound connections)
- id: uuid primary key
- remote_app: text not null
- remote_url: text not null
- api_key_encrypted: text not null
- status: text default 'active' (active|error|pending|disabled)
- entities: text[] default '{}'
- catalog_cache: jsonb
- last_sync: timestamptz
- last_error: text
- created_at: timestamptz

#### integration_logs (all calls)
- id: uuid primary key
- direction: text not null (inbound|outbound)
- method: text not null
- endpoint: text not null
- entity: text
- status_code: int not null
- duration_ms: int not null
- remote_app: text
- created_at: timestamptz

### File Structure
- app/settings/integrations/page.tsx — Admin panel (3 tabs: Keys, Connections, Logs)
- app/api/v1/integration/[...path]/route.ts — Contract endpoints (inbound)
- app/api/integrations/keys/route.ts — CRUD API keys
- app/api/integrations/keys/[id]/route.ts — Revoke key
- app/api/integrations/connections/route.ts — List/create outbound connections
- app/api/integrations/connections/[id]/route.ts — Remove/test connection
- app/api/integrations/discover/route.ts — Discovery proxy
- lib/integration/auth.ts — API key validation middleware
- lib/integration/catalog.ts — App's catalog definition
- lib/integration/helpers.ts — hashKey(), generateKey(), decrypt()
- lib/integration/fetch.ts — integrationFetch() with auto-logging

### Visual Spec
- Dark theme: bg #0A0A0A, surface #111111, text cream #E8E0D5
- Status dots: green (active), yellow (pending), red (error), gray (disabled)
- Method badges: GET sage/green, POST accent/brown, PUT warning/yellow, DELETE danger/red
- Log direction: ↓ blue (inbound), ↑ gold (outbound)
- Fonts: Inter (UI), JetBrains Mono (code/keys), Playfair Display (headings)
- Key display: prefix only (16 chars + ...), copy button on every row
- Expandable rows: collapsed summary → expanded detail with entity toggles/checkboxes

---

## Examples

### Catalog Response
```json
{
  "app": "eximia-forms",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "contract": "eximia-integration/v1",
  "entities": {
    "forms": {
      "operations": ["list", "get"],
      "schema": {
        "id": { "type": "string", "readonly": true },
        "title": { "type": "string", "required": true },
        "status": { "type": "string" },
        "created_at": { "type": "datetime", "readonly": true }
      }
    },
    "submissions": {
      "operations": ["list", "get", "create"],
      "schema": {
        "id": { "type": "string", "readonly": true },
        "form_id": { "type": "string", "required": true },
        "data": { "type": "object", "required": true },
        "created_at": { "type": "datetime", "readonly": true }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Quick Start (Next.js App Router)

1. Create catch-all route: app/api/v1/integration/[...path]/route.ts
2. Define your catalog with entities and schemas
3. Validate x-eximia-api-key header on every request
4. Return paginated responses with { data, meta } format
5. Create admin panel at /settings/integrations

For agents: consume GET /api/spec for the full JSON specification.
